claim_text: The U-shaped distribution of pedestrian intended waiting times emerges from the interaction between vehicles and pedestrians via statistical unconditioning, showing that a large proportion of pedestrians cross immediately while another large proportion wait for the entire red-man phase

The proposed bounded Pareto mixture model better captures pedestrian waiting time behavior at signalized intersections compared to conventional exponential distribution and its extensions, which cannot adequately model the bounded nature and U-shaped pattern of waiting times
Risk-taking pedestrians have significantly smaller intended waiting times than risk-averse pedestrians due to their lower effective critical headway and higher sensitivity to excess headway

Younger pedestrians tend to be risk-takers (higher proportion cross immediately) while elderly pedestrians are more risk-averse (higher proportion wait for entire phase); males are more aggressive than females in crossing behavior
